_comment: Thick-target (alpha,n) neutron yields in OXIDE for actinides, for the spent-fuel (alpha,n)-on-oxygen neutron source (M12). Closes the M9 'SF-only lower bound' gap: per-nuclide (alpha,n) neutrons-per-decay = oxide_n_s_g / specific_activity(Bq/g), summed as S_an(t)=sum yield_an*A_n(t) off the same Bateman solve, ADDED to the SF source. Values are transcribed VERBATIM from PANDA (Reilly/Ensslin/Smith, 'Passive Nondestructive Assay of Nuclear Materials', NUREG/CR-5550, 1991) Chapter 11 'The Origin of Neutron Radiation' (N. Ensslin), the 'Useful Nuclear Data' tables reproduced in the 2007 PANDA Addendum: Table 13 '(alpha,n) Neutron Yields' (the 'Oxide (n/s-g)' column) and Table 14 'Thick-Target Yields from (alpha,n) Reactions' (the oxygen row). The numbers were read from the RENDERED table image (pdftotext -layout scrambled the row/column alignment -- it mispaired e.g. Pu-238 oxide as 8.3e-5; the image gives the correct 1.34e4); every Total-Half-Life entry was checked against the known isotope half-life to confirm the row pairing, and the 'Yield (alpha/s-g)' column was checked against the isotope specific activity (Pu-238 6.4e11 ~= SA 6.34e11) to CONFIRM the oxide column is per gram of ISOTOPE, not per gram of oxide -- so the per-decay denominator must be the isotope specific activity (lambda*N_A/M). See PROVENANCE.md.
source: PANDA / NUREG-CR-5550 (Reilly, Ensslin, Smith, LANL, 1991), Ch.11 'The Origin of Neutron Radiation' (N. Ensslin); Useful Nuclear Data Tables 13 & 14 as reproduced in the 2007 PANDA Addendum (T.D. Reilly), pp. 11-16, 11-17.
basis: per gram of ISOTOPE (confirmed: the table's 'Yield (alpha/s-g)' column equals the isotope specific activity). oxide matrix = MO2 (UO2/PuO2); the (alpha,n) target is the oxygen, ~11.8% by mass identical in UO2 and PuO2 with near-identical U/Pu alpha-stopping, so the per-gram-of-emitter oxide yield transfers from PuO2 to spent UOX fuel.

thick_target_O_yield.table14_value: 0.059 +/- 0.002 neutrons per 1e6 alpha at 5.2 MeV (Pu energies); at 4.7 MeV (U-234) it is 0.040 +/- 0.001
thick_target_O_yield.note_pure_O_upper_bound: This is the PURE-oxygen thick-target yield. In an OXIDE the alpha mostly stops in the heavy metal (high (alpha,n) threshold, no neutrons), so pure-O OVERESTIMATES the oxide (alpha,n) yield by ~2-3x (cf. Pu-238 Table-13 oxide 1.34e4 vs a pure-O reconstruction 6.4e11*5.9e-8 ~ 3.8e4). That is the SAFE (over-count) direction for a residual warning.
thick_target_O_yield.use: ONLY to BOUND (upper) the (alpha,n) of inventory alpha-emitters ABSENT from the Table-13 oxide list (Am-243, Cm-243/245/246/247/248, ...): nominal yield_per_decay = alpha_branch * n_per_alpha. Never enters the modeled dose; sizes the residual dropped-(alpha,n) warning only (an over-estimate, so the true unmodeled fraction is smaller).
